description: Known Java-vs-.NET runtime parity divergences. Entries are matched by operation and optional file, fileGlob, category, or categoryGlob.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #442 closes and malformed/header-offset load parity reaches match status.
entries.ratchet: Known rows for this fixture must not increase; remove this entry when the fixture matches Java.
entries.reason: Parser header recovery differs from Java for this malformed/header-offset fixture, so downstream operations are missing until load parity is fixed.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for rendering rows.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove fixture names as rendering parity improves.
entries.reason: Rendering minimal parser fixtures currently throws before producing Java-compatible blank-page output.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for text rows.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove fixture names as text parity improves.
entries.reason: Text extraction for minimal parser fixtures currently throws before producing Java-compatible text output.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for text extraction.
entries.ratchet: The total known text rows must trend down and must not exceed the checked-in ratchet baseline.
entries.reason: Default PDFTextStripper semantic output still differs from Java for many successful text extraction cases.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for save structural rows.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove fixture names as saved structure matches Java.
entries.reason: Saved .NET output reloads in Java but exposes object-stream/page-tree traversal gaps; Java cannot visit the expected page index after save.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for save structural rows.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove fixture names as saved text/render structure matches Java.
entries.reason: Saved .NET output reloads in Java but loses Java-observable text or first-page render semantics; tracked as remaining semantic parity work.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for rendering rows.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove fixture names as non-placeholder rendering lands.
entries.reason: Current .NET first-page renders are blank or near-uniform while Java renders non-background content; tracked as regression fixtures for render validity.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for rendering rows.
entries.ratchet: The total known render-placeholder rows must trend down and must not exceed the checked-in ratchet baseline.
entries.reason: Placeholder renders are reported separately until PageDrawer and related rendering gaps are fixed.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for rendering rows.
entries.ratchet: The total known render detail mismatches must trend down and must not exceed the checked-in ratchet baseline.
entries.reason: Successful render output may be blank or near-blank until rendering validity checks and PageDrawer work land.

entries.expiresWhen: Issue #441 reaches zero-known runtime parity for pattern color-space rendering.
entries.ratchet: Known rows covered by this entry must not increase; remove this entry when survey.pdf renders like Java.
entries.reason: Pattern color space rendering fails until advanced rendering work is implemented.
